old_claim.note: Sickle Harvesting Tools provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The studied implements are cereal-processing machinery embedded in Early Neolithic farming systems; cultivated cereal production supplies the crop stream they thresh.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and discussion describe threshing sledges as agricultural machinery for processing cereals within Early Neolithic farming systems.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The inferred sledges process cereal crops within established Neolithic farming systems.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Agriculture supplies the relevant production system, while harvesting with a sickle is only one optional upstream collection method.

ontology_before: A specific sickle tradition was treated as the enabler of every threshing operation.
ontology_after: The sledge is linked to the agricultural cereal system documented by the study, without requiring one harvesting implement.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: agriculture replaces sickle_harvesting_tools as the crop-system prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The candidate inserts were shown to process only wild non-agricultural material rather than cereals in Neolithic farming contexts.
short_reason: Cereal agriculture enables the sledge; a sickle is not a required harvesting path.
